_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-28 05:58:31Z)**_

No high-severity issues found, but I see several medium/low risks in the diff for [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx).

1. **Medium:** Click can still trigger default navigation/action while opening popover  
   - [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:214`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:214), [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:229`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:229)  
   - `stopPropagation()` is used without `preventDefault()`. On highlighted links/buttons, user may navigate away instead of just opening the comment anchor UI.

2. **Medium:** `mousemove` path may regress performance at scale  
   - [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:100`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:100), [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:235`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:235)  
   - Repeated range iteration + `getClientRects()` on every move can be expensive with many anchors/long docs.

3. **Low:** Unsafe selector interpolation with raw `commentId`  
   - [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:274`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:274)  
   - `querySelector(\`[data-comment-id="${commentId}"]\`)` can break on selector-special characters. Use `CSS.escape(commentId)`.

4. **Low:** Popover position can go off-screen  
   - [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:291`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:291), [`CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:292`](<REPO_ROOT>/apps/web/src/components/modules/comment/CommentAnchorHighlight.tsx:292)  
   - X appears only max-clamped; Y not fully clamped. On small viewports/bottom edge, popover can render outside viewport.

**Testing gaps**
- Missing interaction tests for open/close behavior and overlap dedup.
- Missing regression test for highlighted interactive elements (link/button click behavior).
- Missing tests for escaped IDs and viewport clamp logic.

**Assumption to confirm**
- If intended UX is “highlight click opens comment popover,” finding #1 should be fixed; if link navigation should win, severity drops.
